title: Synth netlist completely changes depending on non-synthesizable constructs
body:
### Version

Yosys 0.42+15 (git sha1 dd9624444, g++ 11.4.0-1ubuntu1~22.04 -fPIC -Os)

### On which OS did this happen?

Linux

### Reproduction Steps

Run the build steps in [1.corescore_cc](https://github.com/chili-chips-ba/openCologne/tree/main/8.StressTest/1.corescore_cc). Then add the `$display` line in this initial block, in `1.corescore_cc/fusesoc_libraries/fusesoc-cores/serv/serving/serving_ram.v`, and run the build steps again. Compare the netlists.
```
   initial
     if(|memfile) begin
	$display("Preloading %m from %s", memfile);
	$readmemh(memfile, mem);
     end
```
### Expected Behavior

It's expected that the synth netlists stay the same since all the non-synthesizable constructs should not contribute to the netlist of primitives. This difference impeded the Cologne Chip PnR, leading to a decrease in number of cores routable (from 56 to 54) in [1.corescore](https://github.com/chili-chips-ba/openCologne/tree/main/8.StressTest/1.corescore_cc)

### Actual Behavior

The synth netlists change significantly, both provided along with the logs and the rest of the implemenation files as an attachment.

[build.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19042304/build.zip)
